heading: Accessible recreation opportunities
label: 8485 Accessible recreation opportunities

children.text: on National Forest System lands in each region of the Forest Service;
on land managed by the National Park Service in each region of the National Park Service;
on land managed by the Bureau of Land Management in each region of the Bureau of Land Management; and
on land managed by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service in each region of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

children.label: A
children.text: create a new accessible recreation opportunity; or

children.label: B
children.text: modify an existing recreation opportunity into an accessible recreation opportunity; and

children.label: A
children.text: consult with stakeholders with respect to the feasibility and resources necessary for completing the accessible recreation opportunity;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure the accessible recreation opportunity complies with the Architectural Barriers Act of 1968 ( et seq.) and ; and42 U.S.C. 4151section 794 of title 29

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent practicable, ensure that outdoor constructed features supporting the accessible recreation opportunity, including trail bridges, parking spaces and restroom facilities, meet the requirements of the Architectural Barriers Act of 1968 and .section 794 of title 29
camp shelters, camping facilities, and camping units;
hunting, fishing, shooting, or archery ranges or locations;
snow activities, including skiing and snowboarding;
water activities, including kayaking, paddling, canoeing, and boat launch ramps;
rock climbing;
biking;
off-highway vehicle recreation;
picnic facilities and picnic units;
outdoor constructed features; and
any other new or existing recreation opportunities identified in consultation with stakeholders under subsection (b)(2) and consistent with the applicable land management plan.

children.heading: Completion
children.label: d Completion
children.text: Not later than 7 years after , the Secretary concerned, in coordination with stakeholders consulted with under subsection (b)(2), shall complete each accessible recreation opportunity developed under subsection (a).January 4, 2025

children.heading: Maps, signage, and promotional materials
children.label: e Maps, signage, and promotional materials

children.text: publish and distribute maps and install signage, consistent with Architectural Barriers Act accessibility guidelines and ; andsection 794d of title 29
coordinate with stakeholders to leverage any non-Federal resources necessary for the development, stewardship, completion, or promotion of the accessible trail.

children.heading: Conflict avoidance with other uses
children.label: f Conflict avoidance with other uses
A
children.text: the uses in effect before , with respect to any Federal recreational lands and waters on which the accessible recreation opportunity is located; orJanuary 4, 2025

children.label: B
children.text: multiple-use areas in existence on ; andJanuary 4, 2025
complies with all applicable land use and management plans of the Federal recreational lands and waters on which the accessible recreational opportunity is located.

children.heading: Interim report
children.label: 1 Interim report
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Secretary concerned, in coordination with stakeholders and other interested organizations, shall prepare and publish an interim report that lists the accessible recreation opportunities developed under this section during the previous 3 years.January 4, 2025

children.heading: Final report
children.label: 2 Final report
children.text: Not later than 7 years after , the Secretary concerned, in coordination with stakeholders and other interested organizations, shall prepare and publish a final report that lists the accessible recreation opportunities developed under this section.January 4, 2025
